title: Podman is blocking ports
body: ### Bug description - Another Java program on can not start because of blocking port 39146. the program is not in a container. > Web server failed to start. Port 39146 was already in use. ### Operating system 24H2 windows 11 ### Installation Method None ### Version 1.18.0 ### Steps to reproduce - start podman - run testcontainer tests - delete all containers - start another java programm (can't start because of blocking port 39146) (not in podman) `netstat -ano` doesn't show any tasks with open port 39146 if you run `wsl -shutdown` the java webserver can start again ### Relevant log output ```shell > Web server failed to start. Port 39146 was already in use. ``` ### Additional context C:\Users\xxxxxx>wsl --version WSL-Version: 2.4.12.0 Kernelversion: 5.15.167.4-1 WSLg-Version: 1.0.65 MSRDC-Version: 1.2.5716 Direct3D-Version: 1.611.1-81528511 DXCore-Version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release Windows-Version: 10.0.26100.4652

comments.body: workarround: change the port of the webserver for example 8087,50001. but what ports are getting blocked? port 39147 is also blocked

If the port is not bound by podman in the wsl machine then I don't see how this is a podman issue. Also you have multiple wsl Vm's per your output so did you check all of them and did you check if the port is used on the host?

Also please don't provide screenshots of terminal text, copy and paste the output in code blocks instead.
You also didn't specify what podman version you are actually using, you only list podman-desktop.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Luap99 

> If the port is not bound by podman in the wsl machine then I don't see how this is a podman issue.

Actually we don't know, where the problem is coming from. Today someone said, that he had the same problem with docker.  Maybe it is a problem of wsl.

We don't have repeatable steps. I know only if I do `wsl --shutdown` the problem is temporary away.


```
$ podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.4.2
```
